plan.objective: Extend GroveConfig::load with a legacy-migration branch that reads .grove/explore.json, synthesises a GroveConfig (mode=mcp-llm, mapped steering), rewrites to config.json atomically, and emits a deprecation warning.
key_changes: Add private `LegacyExploreRaw` struct in config.rs with old `mode: String` steering key mirroring the pre-S03 explore.json wire shape.
Add private `migrate_from_legacy_explore(root)` fn: reads explore.json, maps old `mode` → Steering, builds GroveConfig, saves atomically, warns stderr.
Extend GroveConfig::load: config.json present → existing path; explore.json present → migration; neither → actionable error (unchanged).
Deprecation warning on stderr directs users to commit config.json and remove explore.json from the repo.
Migration does NOT delete explore.json — grove doctor (later task) will warn on its presence.
No changes to explore/config.rs — ExploreConfig::config_path is reused read-only from config.rs.
Test 5a: legacy explore.json (old mode key) migrates → config.json with mode=McpLlm and correct explore.steering.
Test 5b: second load reads config.json directly; does not re-enter migration; explore.json is unmodified.
Test 5c: config.json present alongside stale explore.json — stale file silently ignored.
Test 5d: deprecation warning path is exercised by the migration.
All existing T01 tests unaffected; cargo clippy -D warnings must be clean.
Material change: version bump required at release — config.json created for all deployed mcp-llm projects on next load.

review_plan.objective: Assessed the plan for migrating legacy .grove/explore.json to .grove/config.json on GroveConfig::load.
key_changes: Verified plan against actual source: GroveConfig, Mode::McpLlm, Steering::from_name, Provider::from_name, ExploreConfig::config_path, and GroveConfig::save all exist and are usable as described.
Three-branch load cascade (config.json -> explore.json migration -> grove init bail) is correct; ExploreConfig already in scope, no new import needed.
AC1/AC2 satisfied: from_name calls validate + remap old mode->steering; migration preserves provider, base_url, model, allowed_tools, tap, trace_retain with no data loss.
AC3 one-time warning is structurally guaranteed: config.json presence routes subsequent loads to branch 1; test 5b confirms idempotency.
AC-to-test mapping complete (5a-5d) plus existing missing_file_actionable_error guards AC4.
ADVISORY: default_trace_retain is private in explore/config.rs (only DEFAULT_TRACE_RETAIN const is pub) - serde default ref won't compile from config.rs; use a local fn or the pub const.
ADVISORY: test 5d stderr capture is fragile (plan admits weak CI-output fallback); recommend a testable warning const + side-effect assertion instead.
ADVISORY: malformed explore.json yields Err rather than the grove init steer - acceptable but document the branch semantics.
findings: Plan is feasible and complete; all 6 acceptance criteria map to concrete steps and tests. Approved with three non-blocking advisory notes.

implementation.objective: Extend GroveConfig::load with a legacy-migration branch that reads .grove/explore.json, synthesises a GroveConfig (mode=McpLlm, mapped steering), rewrites to config.json atomically, and emits a one-time deprecation warning.
key_changes: Made Provider::from_name and Steering::from_name pub(crate) in explore/config.rs to allow use from config.rs.
Added pub(crate) DEPRECATION_WARNING const — named constant makes the warning testable without stderr capture.
Added private LegacyExploreRaw struct mirroring old explore.json wire shape with 'mode' as the steering key.
Added default_legacy_trace_retain() helper delegating to crate::explore::config::DEFAULT_TRACE_RETAIN.
Added migrate_from_legacy_explore(root): reads explore.json, maps mode→Steering and provider→Provider, builds GroveConfig{McpLlm}, validates, saves config.json atomically, emits warning.
Extended GroveConfig::load to three-branch cascade: config.json present → existing path; explore.json present → migration; neither → actionable error.
Test 5a: migrate_legacy_explore_writes_config_json — mode=McpLlm, steering=Balanced (from mode=balanced), config.json exists after migration.
Test 5b: second_load_after_migration_reads_config_not_legacy — second load returns equal config; explore.json mtime unchanged.
Test 5c: config_json_present_ignores_stale_explore_json — config.json wins; stale explore.json silently ignored.
Test 5d: deprecation_warning_emitted — asserts DEPRECATION_WARNING contains key strings; exercises migration side-effect.
All 261 tests pass (155 unit + 77 CLI + 28 integration + 1 doc-test). Clippy -D warnings clean.

code_review.objective: Reviewed the legacy .grove/explore.json → config.json migration branch added to GroveConfig::load.
key_changes: Three-branch load cascade correct: config.json → normal; else explore.json → migrate; else grove init bail (AC1, AC4).
Legacy `mode` key correctly remapped to `steering` via validating Steering::from_name; all other explore fields preserved, no data loss (AC2).
One-time deprecation warning structurally guaranteed — post-migration config.json shadows the migration branch on subsequent loads; test 5b confirms idempotency (AC3).
Prior plan-review advisory resolved: DEFAULT_TRACE_RETAIN is pub const in pub mod config, so default_legacy_trace_retain compiles; from_name fns correctly pub(crate).
ExploreConfig::config_path returns .grove/explore.json and is reused read-only — migration never mutates the legacy file (verified via mtime assert in 5b).
Independently ran cargo test -p grove-cst config → 22 passed 0 failed; all four new tests 5a-5d green.
cargo clippy -p grove-cst --all-targets -- -D warnings clean; both files end with trailing newline (AC6).
Follows established T01 conventions: private LegacyExploreRaw wire struct + from_name validation + atomic save reuse.
Advisory (non-blocking): test 5d asserts on const content rather than capturing stderr — agreed tradeoff.
Advisory (non-blocking): malformed explore.json yields Err rather than grove init steer — intentional and documented.

validation.objective: Validate that GroveConfig::load correctly migrates legacy .grove/explore.json to config.json with mode=McpLlm, maps explore.mode→explore.steering, emits a one-time deprecation warning, and handles all four cascade branches correctly.
key_changes: AC1 PASS: Three-branch load cascade correct — config.json→normal; explore.json→migrate_from_legacy_explore; neither→actionable error. Test 5a confirms migration branch triggers.
AC2 PASS: LegacyExploreRaw.mode mapped via Steering::from_name to ExploreConfig.steering. Test 5a asserts mode='balanced'→Steering::Balanced; all other fields preserved.
AC3 PASS: eprintln!(DEPRECATION_WARNING) in migrate_from_legacy_explore. One-time: post-migration config.json routes to branch 1. Test 5b confirms idempotency via mtime assert.
AC4 PASS: Neither file → bail with 'run `grove init`' message. Pre-existing missing_file_actionable_error test still passes; no regression.
AC5 PASS: All four new tests green — 5a (migration), 5b (idempotency+mtime), 5c (config.json wins over stale explore.json), 5d (const content + path exercised).
AC6 PASS: clippy -D warnings clean; cargo test --release --locked: 261 tests (155+77+28+1), 0 failures; both modified files end with trailing newline.
EDGE CASE PASS: config.json + stale explore.json — test 5c confirms load short-circuits on config.json presence; stale file never opened.
EDGE CASE PASS: explore.json mtime unchanged on second load — mtime assertion in 5b catches any inadvertent write to legacy file.
REGRESSION PASS: legacy_mode_key_rejected in explore/config.rs still passes; ExploreConfig deserializer correctly rejects old 'mode' key.
NOTE: test 5d asserts on DEPRECATION_WARNING const content, not stderr capture — accepted tradeoff; eprintln! call confirmed by code inspection.

approve.objective: Architectural sign-off on the legacy .grove/explore.json to config.json forward-migration added to GroveConfig::load.
key_changes: Three-branch load cascade (config.json normal load / explore.json migrate / neither -> grove init steer) is coherent and satisfies AC1 and AC4.
Reuses established T01 conventions: private LegacyExploreRaw wire struct, validating from_name remaps, existing atomic GroveConfig::save. No parallel config path introduced.
Blast radius contained: only cross-module change is widening Provider/Steering::from_name to pub(crate) plus a pub(crate) DEPRECATION_WARNING const. No public API surface shifts.
One-time warning is structurally guaranteed: post-migration config.json shadows the migration branch. Test 5b mtime assert confirms the legacy file is read-only, never mutated or deleted.
Data preserved: explore.mode -> explore.steering remap plus provider/base_url/model/allowed_tools/tap/trace_retain carried forward with no loss (AC2).

Deployment: version bump required at release -- config.json synthesised on next serve/load for all deployed mcp-llm projects; migration automatic; one-time stderr deprecation warning informs operator.
Follow-up: grove doctor should warn on stale explore.json presence (cleanup intentionally deferred here).
Follow-up: 5d asserts on DEPRECATION_WARNING const content rather than stderr capture -- accepted documented tradeoff.
Full pipeline green: plan, review-plan, implement, review-code, validate all approved; 261 tests pass, clippy -D warnings clean, trailing newlines present.
